english_source:
Determine whether each argument is valid or invalid. If invalid, give a counterexample.

Katie is a human being. Therefore, Katie is smarter than a chimpanzee.
ugf_translation: find out if each talk is right or wrong. if it is wrong, give a thing that shows it is not right. the person we talk about is a person. so the person we talk about is more good at thinking than a big animal.

english_source:
Determine whether each argument is valid or invalid. If invalid, give a counterexample.

Bob is a fireman. Therefore, Bob has put out fires.
ugf_translation: find out if each talk is right or wrong. if it is wrong, give a thing that shows the other way. the first person is a person who makes fires. so the first person has made fires.

english_source:
Determine whether each argument is valid or invalid. If invalid, give a counterexample.

Gerald is a mathematics professor. Therefore, Gerald knows how to teach mathematics.
ugf_translation: find out if each talk is right or wrong. if it is wrong, give a thing that shows it is not right. the first person is a teacher of numbers. so the first person knows how to teach numbers.

english_source:
Determine whether each argument is valid or invalid. If invalid, give a counterexample.

Monica is a French teacher. Therefore, Monica knows how to teach French.
ugf_translation: find out if each talk is right or wrong. if it is wrong, give a thing that shows the other way. the person we talk about is a teacher from a far away place. so the person we talk about knows how to teach from a far away place.

english_source:
Determine whether each argument is valid or invalid. If invalid, give a counterexample.

Bob is taller than Susan. Susan is taller than Frankie. Therefore, Bob is taller than Frankie.
ugf_translation: find out if each talk is right or wrong. if it is wrong, give a thing that shows the other way. the first person is taller than the second person. the second person is taller than the third person. so the first person is taller than the third person.

english_source:
Determine whether each argument is valid or invalid. If invalid, give a counterexample.

Craig loves Linda. Linda loves Monique. Therefore, Craig loves Monique.
ugf_translation: find out if each talk is right or wrong. if it is wrong, give a thing that shows it is not right. the first person loves the second person. the second person loves the third person. so the first person loves the third person.

english_source:
Determine whether each argument is valid or invalid. If invalid, give a counterexample.

Orel Hershizer is a Christian. Therefore, Orel Hershizer communicates with God.
ugf_translation: find out if each talk is right or wrong. if it is wrong, give a thing that shows it is not right. the person we talk about is a person who believes in a god. so the person we talk about talks with a god.

english_source:
Determine whether each argument is valid or invalid. If invalid, give a counterexample.

All Muslims pray to Allah. Muhammad is a Muslim. Therefore, Muhammad prays to Allah.
ugf_translation: find out if each talk is right or wrong. if it is wrong, give a thing that shows the other way. all people who talk to a god talk to a god. the man we talk about is a person who talks to a god. so the man we talk about talks to a god.

english_source:
Determine whether each argument is valid or invalid. If invalid, give a counterexample.

Some protozoa are predators. No protozoa are animals. Therefore, some predators are not animals.
ugf_translation: find out if each talk is right or wrong. if it is wrong, give a thing that shows the other way. some big animals that live in trees are animals that eat other animals. no big animals that live in trees are animals. so some animals that eat other animals are not animals.

english_source:
Determine whether each argument is valid or invalid. If invalid, give a counterexample.

Charlie only barks when he hears a burglar outside. Charlie is barking. Therefore, there must be a burglar outside.
ugf_translation: find out if each talk is right or wrong. if it is wrong, give a thing that shows the other way. the first person only makes a loud sound when he hears a person who takes things outside. the first person is making a loud sound. so there must be a person who takes things outside.

english_source:
Supply the missing premise needed for the conclusion to follow.

Ed rides horses. Therefore, Ed is a cowboy.
ugf_translation: give the missing first idea that is needed for the last idea to come after. the first person rides horses. so the first person is a person who rides a horse.

english_source:
Supply the missing premise needed for the conclusion to follow.

Tom was driving over the speed limit. Therefore, Tom was doing something wrong.
ugf_translation: give the missing first thought needed for the last thought to come after. the man was driving more than the most fast. so the man was doing something wrong.

english_source:
Supply the missing premise needed for the conclusion to follow.

If it is raining then the ground is wet. Therefore, the ground must be wet.
ugf_translation: give the missing first thought needed for the last thought to come after. if it is raining then the ground is wet. so the ground must be wet.

english_source:
Supply the missing premise needed for the conclusion to follow.

All elves drink Guinness, which is why Olaf drinks Guinness.
ugf_translation: give the missing first thought that is needed for the last thought to come after. all people who are not animals drink the drink that people call the drink, which is why the person we talk about drinks the drink that people call the drink.

english_source:
Supply the missing premise needed for the conclusion to follow.

Mark didn’t invite me to homecoming. Instead, he invited his friend Alexia. So he must like Alexia more than me. 31
ugf_translation: give the missing first thought needed for the last thought to come after. the first person did not ask me to come to school. instead, he asked his friend the second person. so he must like the second person more than me.

english_source:
Supply the missing premise needed for the conclusion to follow.

The watch must be broken because every time I have looked at it, the hands have been in the same place.
ugf_translation: give the missing first thought that is needed for the last thought to come after. the watch must be broken because each time i have looked at it, the hands have been in the same place.

english_source:
Supply the missing premise needed for the conclusion to follow.

Olaf drank too much Guinness and fell out of his second story apartment window. Therefore, drinking too much Guinness caused Olaf to injure himself.
ugf_translation: give the missing first thought needed for the last thought to come after. the first person drank too much the second person and fell out of his second story home window. so drinking too much the second person made the first person hurt himself.

english_source:
Supply the missing premise needed for the conclusion to follow.

Mark jumped into the air. Therefore, Mark landed back on the ground.
ugf_translation: give the missing first thought needed for the last thought to come after. the first person jumped into the air. so the first person landed back on the ground.

english_source:
Supply the missing premise needed for the conclusion to follow.

In 2009 in the United States, the net worth of the median white household was $113,149 a year, whereas the net worth of the median black household was $5,677. Therefore, as of 2009, the United States was still a racist nation.
ugf_translation: give the missing idea that was needed for the end idea to come after in the country that has many states the number of money for the middle group of white people was 1 3 4 1 1 year while the number of money for the middle group of black people was 5 6 7 so as of this year the country that has many states was still a country that has many states

english_source:
Supply the missing premise needed for the conclusion to follow.

The temperature of the water is 212 degrees Fahrenheit. Therefore, the water is boiling.
ugf_translation: give the missing first idea that is needed for the last idea to come after. the heat of the water is about two and a half times as hot as the heat of the water. so the water is getting hotter.

english_source:
Supply the missing premise needed for the conclusion to follow.

Capital punishment sometimes takes innocent lives, such as the lives of individuals who were later found to be not guilty. Therefore, we should not allow capital punishment.
ugf_translation: give the missing first thought that is needed for the last thought to come after. big bad thing sometimes takes good people, like the lives of people who later were found to be not bad. so we should not let big bad thing.

english_source:
Supply the missing premise needed for the conclusion to follow.

Allowing immigrants to migrate to the U.S. will take working class jobs away from working class folks. Therefore, we should not allow immigrants to migrate to the U.S.
ugf_translation: give the missing idea that is needed for the end idea to come after. let people who come from other places move to the big country will take jobs that work in a class away from people who work in a class. so we should not let people who come from other places move to the big country.

english_source:
Supply the missing premise needed for the conclusion to follow.

Prostitution is a fair economic exchange between two consenting adults. Therefore, prostitution should be allowed.
ugf_translation: give the missing first thought that is needed for the last thought to come after. the act of giving money to a group is a good money give and take between two old people who agree. so the act of giving money to a group should be allowed.

english_source:
Supply the missing premise needed for the conclusion to follow.

Colleges are more interested in making money off of their football athletes than in educating them. Therefore, college football ought to be banned.
ugf_translation: give the missing first thought needed for the last thought to come after. schools are more interested in making money from their ball game people than in teaching them. so school ball game people should not be allowed.

english_source:
Supply the missing premise needed for the conclusion to follow.

Edward received an F in college Algebra. Therefore, Edward should have studied more.
ugf_translation: give the missing first thought that is needed for the last thought to come after. the man got a good mark in school number work. so the man should have studied more.

english_source:
Identify which rhetorical technique (assuring, guarding, discounting, or evaluative language) is being used.

Although drilling for oil in Alaska will disrupt some wildlife, it is better than having to depend on foreign oil, which has the tendency to draw us into foreign conflicts that we would otherwise not be involved in.
ugf_translation: find which way of talking like making people feel safe, keeping people safe, saying things are not important, or using words that say if something is good or bad is being used. even though making a hole for the black stuff in the far north land will break some animals, it is better than having to use the black stuff from other places, which often makes us go into other fights that we would not be part of.

english_source:
Identify which rhetorical technique (assuring, guarding, discounting, or evaluative language) is being used.

Let there be no doubt: the entity that carried out this attack is a known terrorist organization, whose attacks have a characteristic style—a style that is seen in this attack today.
ugf_translation: find which way of talking that makes people feel safe, keeping safe, saying things are not important, or using words that say if something is good or bad is being used. let there be no not sure: the thing that did this attack is a known group that hurts people, whose attacks have a way that is seen in this attack today.

english_source:
Identify which rhetorical technique (assuring, guarding, discounting, or evaluative language) is being used.

Privatizing the water utilities in Detroit was an unprecedented move that has garnered a lot of criticism. Nonetheless, it is helping Detroit to recover from bankruptcy.
ugf_translation: find which way of talking that tries to make people feel safe, that tries to keep safe, that tries to make people think less, or that uses words that say if something is good or bad is being used. making the water things in the city we call the big city not open was a move that no one has seen before that has made many people say bad things about it. still it is helping the city we call the big city get back to the place where people have no money.

english_source:
Identify which rhetorical technique (assuring, guarding, discounting, or evaluative language) is being used.

Most pediatricians agree that the single most important factor in childhood obesity is eating sugary, processed foods, which have become all too common in our day and age.
ugf_translation: find which way of talking that tries to make people feel safe, that tries to keep people safe, that tries to say something is not important, or that tries to say something is good or bad is being used. most people who study children agree that the one most important thing in a time when a child is very heavy is eating sweet food that is made by people, which have become all too many in our day and age.

english_source:
Identify which rhetorical technique (assuring, guarding, discounting, or evaluative language) is being used.

Although not every case of AIDS is caused by HIV, it is arguable that most are.
ugf_translation: find which way of talking that tries to make people feel safe, that tries to keep people safe, that tries to say something is not important, or that uses words that say if something is good or bad is being used. even if not every time the sick thing is made by the sick thing, it can be talked about as true that most are.

english_source:
Identify which rhetorical technique (assuring, guarding, discounting, or evaluative language) is being used.

Abraham Lincoln was probably our greatest president since he helped keep together a nation on the brink of splintering into two.
ugf_translation: find which way of talking that tries to make people feel safe, that tries to keep people safe, that tries to say something is not important, or that uses words that say if something is good or bad is being used. the man who was the head of a country was probably the most important leader since he helped keep a country together on the end of breaking into two parts.

english_source:
Identify which rhetorical technique (assuring, guarding, discounting, or evaluative language) is being used.

No one with any sense would support Obamacare.
ugf_translation: find which way of talking that tries to make people feel safe, that tries to keep people safe, that tries to make people not count, or that uses words that say if something is good or bad is being used. no one with any sense would say yes to the idea that all people who run the country should get help.

english_source:
Identify which rhetorical technique (assuring, guarding, discounting, or evaluative language) is being used.

Even if universal healthcare is expensive, it is still the just thing to do. 36
ugf_translation: find which way of talking that tries to make people feel safe, that tries to keep people safe, that tries to say something is not important, or that tries to say something is good or bad is being used. even if all body care needs a lot of money, it is still the right thing to do.

english_source:
Identify which rhetorical technique (assuring, guarding, discounting, or evaluative language) is being used.

While our country has made significant strides in overcoming explicit racist policies, the wide disparity of wealth, prestige and influence that characterize white and black Americans shows that we are still implicitly a racist country.
ugf_translation: find which way of talking that tries to make people feel safe, keep safe, ignore, or say good or bad is being used. while our country has made big moves in beating clear bad ideas, the big not same of money, good name, and power that talk about white and black people in the country shows that we are still a country that is not said to be a country that is not said to be a country.

english_source:
Identify which rhetorical technique (assuring, guarding, discounting, or evaluative language) is being used.

Recent studies have show that there is no direct link between vaccines and autism.
ugf_translation: find which way of talking that tries to make people feel safe, that tries to keep safe, that tries to say something is not important, or that uses words that say if something is good or bad is being used. new looks have shown that there is no clear join between the thing that helps stop sick and the thing that makes people not well.

english_source:
Identify what kind of problem, if any, the statistical generalization has.

Bob, from Silverton, CO drives a 4x4 pickup truck, so most people from Silverton, CO drive 4x4 pickup trucks.
ugf_translation: find what kind of problem, if any, the big idea about many things has. the first person, from the place we call the first place, the air that comes from burning things makes a big car with four parts, so most people from the place we call the first place, the air that comes from burning things make a big car with four parts.

english_source:
Identify what kind of problem, if any, the statistical generalization has.

Tom counts and categorizes birds that land in the tree in his backyard every morning from 5:00-5:20 am. He counts mostly Mourning Doves and generalizes, “most birds that land in my tree in the morning are Mourning Doves.”
ugf_translation: find what kind of problem, if any, the big idea about many things has. the man counts and puts birds that land in the tree in his outside area every morning from the time when the sun goes down and the time when the sun goes down and the time when the sun goes down and the time when the sun goes down. he counts most birds that land in my tree in the morning are birds that live in the trees.
